title: Prajñāpāramitāhṛdaya Synoptic Alignment

witness_descriptions.T251: Xuanzang's Xīnjīng (ca. 649-656 CE), canonical short version
witness_descriptions.T250: Dàmíngzhòujīng, attributed to Kumārajīva, possibly earlier tradition
witness_descriptions.Fangshan: Fangshan Stele (661 CE), oldest dated physical witness
witness_descriptions.T256: Bilingual text with Sanskrit transliterated in Chinese characters, Tang dynasty
witness_descriptions.T257: Dānapāla's translation (1005 CE) from Sanskrit long recension, only Chinese version with oṃ in mantra
witness_descriptions.S.2464: Dunhuang manuscript (Stein Collection, British Library), 7th-8th c., T251-type
witness_descriptions.S.700: Dunhuang manuscript (Stein Collection), 7th-8th c., T250-type with pre-Xuanzang readings
witness_descriptions.P.2168: Dunhuang manuscript (Pelliot Collection, BnF), 8th-9th c., T251-type
witness_descriptions.P.2323: Dunhuang manuscript dated 851 CE (Pelliot Collection), securely dated witness
witness_descriptions.P.3917: Dunhuang bilingual manuscript (Pelliot Collection), Sanskrit in Chinese transliteration
witness_descriptions.GRETIL: Sanskrit text based on Conze (1967) with corrections
witness_descriptions.Horyuji: Hōryūji palm-leaf manuscript (7th-8th c.), Conze's 'Ja', one of oldest Sanskrit witnesses
witness_descriptions.Na: Nepalese manuscript, Cambridge Add. 1164, ca. 11th c., short recension
witness_descriptions.Nb: Nepalese manuscript, Cambridge Add. 1165, ca. 17th-18th c., long recension with oṃ
witness_descriptions.Nc: Nepalese manuscript, Royal Asiatic Society, ca. 19th c., long recension
witness_descriptions.Nd: Nepalese manuscript, India Office Library, ca. 18th-19th c., long recension
witness_descriptions.Ne: Nepalese manuscript, Nepal-German Project, ca. 15th-17th c., long recension with oṃ
witness_descriptions.Toh21: Degé Kangyur, Tibetan long version with frame narrative
witness_descriptions.D: Degé (Derge) Kangyur, 1733 CE, standard reference edition
witness_descriptions.J: Lithang Kangyur, 1609-1621 CE, earliest printed edition
witness_descriptions.S: Stog Palace manuscript, 14th-15th c., one of oldest Kangyur MSS

chinese.text: 觀自在菩薩，行深般若波羅蜜多時，照見五蘊皆空，度一切苦厄。
chinese.pinyin: Guānzìzài púsà, xíng shēn bōrě bōluómìduō shí, zhàojiàn wǔyùn jiē kōng, dù yīqiè kǔ è.

sanskrit.iast: Ārya-avalokiteśvaro bodhisattvo gambhīrāṃ prajñāpāramitācaryāṃ caramāṇo vyavalokayati sma: pañca-skandhās, tāṃś ca svabhāva-śūnyān paśyati sma.
sanskrit.devanagari: आर्य-अवलोकितेश्वरो बोधिसत्त्वो गम्भीरां प्रज्ञापारमिताचर्यां चरमाणो व्यवलोकयति स्म: पञ्च-स्कन्धास्, तांश्च स्वभाव-शून्यान् पश्यति स्म।

tibetan.wylie: yang de'i tshe byang chub sems dpa' sems dpa' chen po 'phags pa spyan ras gzigs dbang phyug shes rab kyi pha rol tu phyin pa zab mo'i spyod pa nyid la rnam par blta zhing / phung po lnga po de dag la yang rang bzhin gyis stong par rnam par blta'o/

rows.english: When Avalokiteśvara Bodhisattva was practicing the profound prajñāpāramitā, he illuminated the five aggregates and saw that they are all empty, thus transcending all suffering.
divergence_notes: Sanskrit: vyavalokayati sma - imperfect tense unusual for sūtra style
Tibetan preserves 'at that time' (yang de'i tshe) temporal marker absent in T251
Section I error (Attwood 2015): Conze's pañca-skandhās (nominative) should be pañcaskandhāṃs (accusative) - skandhas are objects of vyavalokayati ('examined')

chinese.text: 舍利子，色不異空，空不異色；色即是空，空即是色。受想行識亦復如是。
chinese.pinyin: Shèlìzǐ, sè bù yì kōng, kōng bù yì sè; sè jí shì kōng, kōng jí shì sè. Shòu xiǎng xíng shí yì fù rúshì.

sanskrit.iast: Iha Śāriputra rūpaṃ śūnyatā śūnyataiva rūpam, rūpān na pṛthak śūnyatā śūnyatāyā na pṛthag rūpam, yad rūpaṃ sā śūnyatā yā śūnyatā tad rūpam; evam eva vedanā-saṃjñā-saṃskāra-vijñānāni.
sanskrit.devanagari: इह शारिपुत्र रूपं शून्यता शून्यतैव रूपम्, रूपान् न पृथक् शून्यता शून्यतायाः न पृथग् रूपम्, यद् रूपं सा शून्यता या शून्यता तद् रूपम्; एवमेव वेदना-संज्ञा-संस्कार-विज्ञानानि।

tibetan.text: གཟུགས་སྟོང་པའོ། སྟོང་པ་ཉིད་གཟུགས་སོ། གཟུགས་ལས་སྟོང་པ་ཉིད་གཞན་མ་ཡིན། སྟོང་པ་ཉིད་ལས་ཀྱང་གཟུགས་གཞན་མ་ཡིན་ནོ། དེ་བཞིན་དུ་ཚོར་བ་དང་། འདུ་ཤེས་དང་། འདུ་བྱེད་དང་། རྣམ་པར་ཤེས་པ་རྣམས་སྟོང་པའོ།
tibetan.wylie: gzugs stong pa'o/ stong pa nyid gzugs so/ gzugs las stong pa nyid gzhan ma yin/ stong pa nyid las kyang gzugs gzhan ma yin no/ de bzhin du tshor ba dang / 'du shes dang / 'du byed dang / rnam par shes pa rnams stong pa'o/

rows.english: Śāriputra, form is not different from emptiness, emptiness is not different from form; form is emptiness, emptiness is form. Feeling, perception, formation, and consciousness are also like this.
divergence_notes: Sanskrit 'yad rūpaṃ sā śūnyatā yā śūnyatā tad rūpam' has no Chinese counterpart - late interpolation (Nattier 1992, Attwood 2017a)
Original Aṣṭa reading was 'rūpam eva māyā māyaiva rūpam' (appearance is only illusion)

chinese.text: 舍利子，是諸法空相，不生不滅，不垢不淨，不增不減。
chinese.pinyin: Shèlìzǐ, shì zhūfǎ kōng xiāng, bù shēng bù miè, bù gòu bù jìng, bù zēng bù jiǎn.

sanskrit.iast: Iha Śāriputra sarva-dharmāḥ śūnyatā-lakṣaṇā, anutpannā aniruddhā, amalā avimalā, anūnā aparipūrṇāḥ.
sanskrit.devanagari: इह शारिपुत्र सर्व-धर्माः शून्यता-लक्षणा, अनुत्पन्ना अनिरुद्धा, अमला अविमला, अनूना अपरिपूर्णाः।

tibetan.text: ཤཱ་རིའི་བུ། དེ་ལྟར་ཆོས་ཐམས་ཅད་སྟོང་པ་ཉིད་དེ། མཚན་ཉིད་མེད་པ། མ་སྐྱེས་པ། མ་འགགས་པ། དྲི་མ་མེད་པ། དྲི་མ་དང་བྲལ་བ་མེད་པ། བྲི་བ་མེད་པ། གང་བ་མེད་པའོ།
tibetan.wylie: shA ri'i bu/ de ltar chos thams cad stong pa nyid de/ mtshan nyid med pa/ ma skyes pa/ ma 'gags pa/ dri ma med pa/ dri ma dang bral ba med pa/ bri ba med pa/ gang ba med pa'o/

rows.english: Śāriputra, the emptiness characteristic of all dharmas is: not arising, not ceasing; not defiled, not pure; not increasing, not decreasing.

chinese.text: 是故空中，無色，無受想行識，
chinese.pinyin: Shìgù kōng zhōng, wú sè, wú shòu xiǎng xíng shí,

sanskrit.iast: Tasmāc Chāriputra śūnyatāyāṃ na rūpaṃ na vedanā na saṃjñā na saṃskārāḥ na vijñānam.
sanskrit.devanagari: तस्माच् छारिपुत्र शून्यतायां न रूपं न वेदना न संज्ञा न संस्काराः न विज्ञानम्।

tibetan.text: ཤཱ་རིའི་བུ། དེ་ལྟ་བས་ན་སྟོང་པ་ཉིད་ལ་གཟུགས་མེད། ཚོར་བ་མེད། འདུ་ཤེས་མེད། འདུ་བྱེད་རྣམས་མེད། རྣམ་པར་ཤེས་པ་མེད།
tibetan.wylie: shA ri'i bu/ de lta bas na stong pa nyid la gzugs med/ tshor ba med/ 'du shes med/ 'du byed rnams med/ rnam par shes pa med/

rows.english: Therefore, in emptiness there is no form, no feeling, perception, formation, or consciousness;

chinese.text: 無眼耳鼻舌身意，無色聲香味觸法，
chinese.pinyin: wú yǎn ěr bí shé shēn yì, wú sè shēng xiāng wèi chù fǎ,

tibetan.text: མིག་མེད། རྣ་བ་མེད། སྣ་མེད། ལྕེ་མེད། ལུས་མེད། ཡིད་མེད། གཟུགས་མེད། སྒྲ་མེད། དྲི་མེད། རོ་མེད། རེག་བྱ་མེད། ཆོས་མེད་དོ།
tibetan.wylie: mig med/ rna ba med/ sna med/ lce med/ lus med/ yid med/ gzugs med/ sgra med/ dri med/ ro med/ reg bya med/ chos med do/

rows.english: no eyes, ears, nose, tongue, body, or mind; no forms, sounds, smells, tastes, tangibles, or mental objects;

chinese.pinyin: wú yǎn jiè, nǎizhì wú yìshí jiè.

tibetan.wylie: mig gi khams med pa nas yid kyi khams med/ yid kyi rnam par shes pa'i khams kyi bar du yang med do/

rows.english: no eye-element, up to and including no mind-consciousness element.

chinese.text: 無無明，亦無無明盡，乃至無老死，亦無老死盡。
chinese.pinyin: Wú wúmíng, yì wú wúmíng jìn, nǎizhì wú lǎosǐ, yì wú lǎosǐ jìn.

sanskrit.iast: Na-avidyā na-avidyā-kṣayo yāvan na jarā-maraṇaṃ na jarā-maraṇa-kṣayo.
sanskrit.devanagari: न-अविद्या न-अविद्या-क्षयो यावन् न जरा-मरणं न जरा-मरण-क्षयो।

tibetan.wylie: ma rig pa med/ ma rig pa zad pa med pa nas/ rga shi med/ rga shi zad pa'i bar du yang med do/

rows.english: No ignorance and no ending of ignorance, up to and including no old age and death, and no ending of old age and death.
divergence_notes: Sanskrit uses kṣaya (destruction/exhaustion) instead of standard nirodha (cessation)
Tibetan zad pa (exhaustion) matches Sanskrit kṣaya - evidence of back-translation (Nattier 1992)
Chinese 盡 (jìn) is ambiguous between kṣaya and nirodha readings

chinese.text: 無苦集滅道，無智亦無得，以無所得故。
chinese.pinyin: Wú kǔ jí miè dào, wú zhì yì wú dé, yǐ wú suǒ dé gù.

sanskrit.iast: Na duḥkha-samudaya-nirodha-mārgā. Na jñānaṃ, na prāptir na-aprāptiḥ.
sanskrit.devanagari: न दुःख-समुदय-निरोध-मार्गा। न ज्ञानं, न प्राप्तिर् न-अप्राप्तिः।

tibetan.text: སྡུག་བསྔལ་བ་དང་། ཀུན་འབྱུང་བ་དང་། འགོག་པ་དང་། ལམ་མེད། ཡེ་ཤེས་མེད། ཐོབ་པ་མེད། མ་ཐོབ་པ་ཡང་མེད་དོ།
tibetan.wylie: sdug bsngal ba dang / kun 'byung ba dang / 'gog pa dang / lam med/ ye shes med/ thob pa med/ ma thob pa yang med do/

rows.english: No suffering, origination, cessation, or path; no wisdom and no attainment, because there is nothing to attain.
divergence_notes: Sanskrit na-aprāptiḥ is late interpolation not in Large Sutra (Huifeng 2014, Attwood 2020a)
Should be 'na prāptir nābhisamayaḥ' (no attainment, no realization) - refers to mārga/phala (path/fruition)
Chinese 無智亦無得 reflects Kumārajīva's T223 error copied into Heart Sutra
Tibetan ma thob pa yang med do ('no non-attainment') follows Sanskrit interpolation

chinese.text: 菩提薩埵，依般若波羅蜜多故。心無罣礙，無罣礙故。無有恐怖，遠離顚倒夢想，究竟涅槃。
chinese.pinyin: Pútísàduǒ, yī bōrě bōluómìduō gù. Xīn wú guà'ài, wú guà'ài gù. Wú yǒu kǒngbù, yuǎnlí diāndǎo mèngxiǎng, jiūjìng nièpán.

sanskrit.iast: Tasmāc Chāriputra aprāptitvād bodhisattvasya prajñāpāramitām āśritya viharaty acittāvaraṇaḥ. Cittāvaraṇa-nāstitvād atrastro viparyāsa-atikrānto niṣṭhā-nirvāṇa-prāptaḥ.
sanskrit.devanagari: तस्माच् छारिपुत्र अप्राप्तित्वाद् बोधिसत्त्वस्य प्रज्ञापारमितामाश्रित्य विहरत्यचित्तावरणः। चित्तावरण-नास्तित्वाद् अत्रस्त्रो विपर्यास-अतिक्रान्तो निष्ठा-निर्वाण-प्राप्तः।

tibetan.wylie: shA ri'i bu/ de lta bas na byang chub sems dpa' rnams thob pa med pa'i phyir/ shes rab kyi pha rol tu phyin pa la brten cing gnas te/ sems la sgrib pa med pas 'jigs pa med de/ phyin ci log las shin tu 'das nas mya ngan las 'das pa'i mthar phyin to/

rows.english: Because bodhisattvas rely on prajñāpāramitā, their minds are without obstruction. Without obstruction, they have no fear, are far removed from inverted views and dreams, and attain ultimate nirvāṇa.
divergence_notes: Section VI error (Attwood 2018a, 2020a): Conze's wrongly placed full stop creates sentence fragment
niṣṭhānirvāṇaḥ is bahuvrīhi compound (masc. adj.) meaning 'one whose nirvāṇa is complete' - describes the bodhisattva
Corrected reading: 'viharaty acittāvaraṇaś cittāvaraṇanāstitvād atrasto viparyāsātikrānto niṣṭhānirvāṇaḥ'
Tibetan mya ngan las 'das pa'i mthar phyin (attain the final nirvāṇa) matches Sanskrit structure

chinese.text: 三世諸佛，依般若波羅蜜多故，得阿耨多羅三藐三菩提。
chinese.pinyin: Sānshì zhū fó, yī bōrě bōluómìduō gù, dé ānòuduōluó sānmiǎo sānpútí.

sanskrit.iast: Tryadhva-vyavasthitāḥ sarva-buddhāḥ prajñāpāramitām āśrityānuttarāṃ samyak-saṃbodhim abhisaṃbuddhāḥ.

tibetan.wylie: dus gsum du rnam par bzhugs pa'i sangs rgyas thams cad kyang shes rab kyi pha rol tu phyin pa la brten nas bla na med pa yang dag par rdzogs pa'i byang chub tu mngon par rdzogs par sangs rgyas so/

rows.english: All buddhas of the three times, relying on prajñāpāramitā, attain anuttarā-samyak-saṃbodhi.

chinese.text: 故知般若波羅蜜多，是大神咒，是大明咒，是無上咒，是無等等咒，能除一切苦，眞實不虛故。
chinese.pinyin: Gùzhī bōrě bōluómìduō, shì dà shén zhòu, shì dà míng zhòu, shì wúshàng zhòu, shì wú děngděng zhòu, néng chú yīqiè kǔ, zhēnshí bù xū gù.

sanskrit.iast: Tasmāj jñātavyam: prajñāpāramitā mahā-mantro mahā-vidyā-mantro 'nuttara-mantro 'samasama-mantraḥ, sarva-duḥkha-praśamanaḥ, satyam amithyatvāt.
sanskrit.devanagari: तस्माज् ज्ञातव्यम्: प्रज्ञापारमिता महा-मन्त्रो महा-विद्या-मन्त्रो ऽनुत्तर-मन्त्रो ऽसमसम-मन्त्रः, सर्व-दुःख-प्रशमनः, सत्यमम् इथ्यत्वात्।

tibetan.wylie: de lta bas na shes rab kyi pha rol tu phyin pa'i sngags/ rig pa chen po'i sngags/ bla na med pa'i sngags/ mi mnyam pa dang mnyam pa'i sngags/ sdug bsngal thams cad rab tu zhi bar byed pa'i sngags/ mi rdzun pas na bden par shes par bya ste/ shes rab kyi pha rol tu phyin pa'i sngags smras pa/

rows.english: Therefore know that prajñāpāramitā is the great mantra, the mantra of great illumination, the supreme mantra, the unequalled mantra, which can remove all suffering; it is true and not false.

chinese.text: 說般若波羅蜜多咒，卽說咒曰：揭帝揭帝，波羅揭帝，波羅僧揭帝，菩提僧莎訶。
chinese.pinyin: Shuō bōrě bōluómìduō zhòu, jí shuō zhòu yuē: Jiēdì jiēdì, bōluó jiēdì, bōluó sēng jiēdì, pútí sēng suōhē.

sanskrit.iast: Prajñāpāramitāyām ukto mantraḥ. Tadyathā: Oṃ gate gate pāragate pārasaṃgate bodhi svāhā.
sanskrit.devanagari: प्रज्ञापारमितायामुक्तो मन्त्रः। तद्यथा: ॐ गते गते पारगते पारसंगते बोधि स्वाहा।

tibetan.wylie: tad+ya thA/ oM ga te ga te pA ra ga te pA ra saM ga te bo d+hi swA hA/

rows.english: Therefore proclaim the prajñāpāramitā mantra, proclaim the mantra that says: Gate gate pāragate pārasaṃgate bodhi svāhā.

notes: Synoptic alignment follows Chinese compositional priority methodology (Nattier 1992)
T251 (short version) serves as base text; Tibetan Toh21 (long version) includes frame narrative not shown in main alignment
Tibetan segments extracted from Toh21 correspond to doctrinal core matching T251 structure
Sanskrit textual corrections based on Attwood (2015, 2017a, 2017b, 2018a, 2020a, 2021) and Huifeng (2014)
Gilgit Pañcaviṃśatisāhasrikā (Karashima et al. 2016) provides key Large Sutra parallels for textual analysis
T250 (Dàmíngzhòujīng) preserves possibly earlier Chinese tradition with additional passages and three-epithet pattern
Fangshan Stele (661 CE) is oldest dated witness; virtually identical to T251 with minor orthographic variants
T256 provides Sanskrit text in Chinese character transliteration, crucial for Tang-era pronunciation and confirming Sanskrit tradition
T257 (Dānapāla 1005 CE) is the only Chinese version with oṃ in mantra; translated from elaborated Sanskrit long recension with frame narrative
Hōryūji manuscript (7th-8th c.) is one of oldest Sanskrit witnesses; lacks oṃ in mantra and na-aprāptiḥ, supporting late interpolation thesis
Nepalese manuscripts (Na-Ne, 11th-19th c.) represent late Tantric elaboration with maṅgala, oṃ in mantra, and frame narrative; should not be privileged over early witnesses
Dunhuang manuscripts (Stein and Pelliot collections) provide early witnesses from 7th-9th c.; both T251-type and T250-type found
P.2323 (dated 851 CE) is a securely dated Dunhuang witness; S.700 preserves pre-Xuanzang T250 tradition
Multiple Tibetan Kangyur editions (Degé, Peking, Narthang, Lhasa, Lithang, Stog) collated for variant readings
Tibetan zad pa (exhaustion) matches Sanskrit kṣaya, supporting back-translation hypothesis
See data/collation/variant_table.json for detailed collation of key textual variants across all witnesses
See data/stemma/stemma_codicum.json for manuscript relationships and transmission history
scholarly_refs: Nattier, Jan (1992). 'The Heart Sūtra: a Chinese apocryphal text?' JIABS 15(2): 153-223.
